edge_hypothesis: Advisory data reveals ETH/1h has ranging-regime PF=1.115 — the highest regime-specific edge in the entire dataset. Bollinger breakouts are designed to capture volatility expansion from compression (squeeze). This is a fundamentally different thesis from all pipeline specs: instead of following existing trends, we catch the moment a range BREAKS. Volume confirmation (1.2-1.5x average) filters false breakouts. The key insight is regime-targeting: by designing explicitly for ranging-to-expansion transitions on ETH/1h, we align strategy mechanics with the strongest observed regime edge. Additionally, Bollinger breakouts are structurally infrequent (bands must be broken, not just touched), naturally limiting trade count and fee drag.
regime_assumption: RANGING-TO-EXPANSION. This strategy specifically targets the transition from low-volatility ranging into directional expansion. It will fire when Bollinger bands are broken, which happens most often during range breakouts. Expected to underperform during sustained trends (price stays outside bands, no new breakout signal). Expected to fail during false breakouts in choppy conditions. Invalidate if ranging-regime PF < 1.0 (contradicts the ETH/1h ranging edge finding) or if false breakout rate exceeds 70%.
invalidation_conditions: Ranging-regime PF < 1.0 (should specifically exploit ranging edge)
False breakout rate > 70% (volume filter not working)
Total trades < 15 (signal too rare to be testable)
Fee drag > 40% of gross profit (frequency too high despite being breakout-only)

variants.name: boll_squeeze_vol_confirm
variants.description: Bollinger breakout with volume confirmation (1.2x avg) on ETH/1h. Core thesis test: does capturing ranging-to-expansion transitions on ETH/1h exploit the 1.115 ranging PF edge? Volume filter aims to distinguish real breakouts from noise.
entry_long: Close breaks above BBU_20_2.0 (upper Bollinger band breakout).
entry_short: Close breaks below BBL_20_2.0 (lower Bollinger band breakdown).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime target: Ranging-to-expansion transitions (ETH/1h ranging PF=1.115)
exit_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR below entry (tight: breakout should move immediately).
Take profit at 2.5x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.67).
Exit if price re-enters Bollinger band (breakout failed).
Exit on max 15 bars in trade (breakout momentum is short-lived on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: False breakout with immediate reversal into range
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Volume spike without follow-through (news-driven)
risk_rules: Reduced risk for 1h — higher frequency means more fee exposure.

variants.name: boll_breakout_high_vol
variants.description: Bollinger breakout requiring stronger volume confirmation (1.5x avg). Tests whether stricter volume gate reduces false breakouts enough to improve PF, even at cost of fewer trades.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime target: Strong volume breakouts from range
exit_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR below entry.
Take profit at 3.0x ATR above entry (R:R 1:2.0 — wider target for high-conviction entries).
Exit if price re-enters Bollinger band.
Exit on max 20 bars in trade.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Volume gate too strict — insufficient trades
Hypothesis failure mode guard: High-volume breakout reversal (stop hunt)
risk_rules: Higher conviction entries allow standard risk allocation.

components.notes: High volume ratio (1.5x) as conviction filter

variants.name: boll_breakout_4h_trend
variants.description: Bollinger breakout on ETH/4h. Tests whether band breakouts on higher timeframe produce higher-quality, lower-frequency signals. 4h breakouts should be more structural than 1h noise.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime target: 4h structural breakouts from compression
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR below entry (wider for 4h volatility).
Take profit at 3.5x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.75).
Exit if price re-enters Bollinger band.
Exit on max 30 bars in trade (5 days on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 4h breakouts too infrequent for meaningful sample
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Trending regime dominates 4h — breakouts redundant
